hypothesis: If MatAgent's integrated approach (LLM-driven planning and proposition with external tools, DiffCSP-based crystal-structure generation, and iComFormer energy evaluation) is used to iteratively explore compositions and generate structures toward target formation energies, then the resulting candidates will achieve a higher success rate within ±0.25 eV/atom of the target and exhibit greater compositional validity, uniqueness, and novelty than tool-free LLM baselines and MatterGen, because deliberate planning, constrained generation, and feedback-driven refinement steer exploration toward energy regions that satisfy the target.
